entries.name: hautelook/phpass
entries.description: In May 2022 an attacker claimed the abandoned upstream GitHub repository behind the Packagist package hautelook/phpass (2.5M+ lifetime downloads) and published malicious versions. The altered PasswordHash.php read the AWS_ACCESS_KEY and AWS_SECRET_KEY environment variables and POSTed them to an attacker Heroku endpoint (https://anti-theft-web.herokuapp.com/hacked/) — the same campaign that backdoored the PyPI package 'ctx'.
indicators: PasswordHash.php reads AWS_ACCESS_KEY / AWS_SECRET_KEY env vars
upstream GitHub repo ownership recently transferred

entries.name: laravel-lang/actions
entries.description: On 2026-05-22 an attacker with push access to the Laravel-Lang GitHub organization rewrote 46 tags of laravel-lang/actions within ~15 minutes, injecting an RCE backdoor. The malicious commits added src/helpers.php to the Composer autoload.files section so the payload ran automatically whenever vendor/autoload.php loaded, exfiltrating CI secrets and environment variables to a typosquatted attacker domain and removing artifacts within seconds to hinder forensics.
indicators: git tags force-rewritten across the package history
exfiltrates CI secrets to a typosquatted domain

entries.name: symfont/process
entries.description: Typosquat of the widely used symfony/process component, disclosed September 2021. The malicious 'symfont/process' package impersonated the Symfony Process library to trick developers into installing attacker-controlled code via a one-character vendor-name change.

entries.name: nhattuanbl/lara-helper
entries.description: PHP package by threat actor 'nhattuanbl' disguised as a Laravel helper utility; Socket flagged it as a fully-featured RAT/backdoor. After clean packages built install history under the same author (June-Dec 2024), the malicious version shipped a remote-access payload: a 60s heartbeat plus operators cmd (shell), powershell, download (read file as base64), upload (write file from base64) and screenshot (imagegrabscreen). C2 helper.leuleu.net:2096 over AES-128-CTR with a hardcoded key; retries every 15s indefinitely.
indicators: RAT commands: cmd, powershell, download, upload, screenshot
AES-128-CTR C2 to helper.leuleu.net:2096 with a hardcoded key

entries.name: nhattuanbl/simple-queue
entries.description: Second malicious package in the 'nhattuanbl' Laravel-utility campaign (Socket, June-Dec 2024). Carries the same RAT payload as nhattuanbl/lara-helper — heartbeat plus cmd/powershell/download/upload/screenshot operators. C2 helper.leuleu.net:2096 over AES-128-CTR with a hardcoded key; retries every 15s indefinitely.
indicators: embedded PHP RAT identical to lara-helper

entries.name: nhattuanbl/lara-swagger
entries.description: Third package in the 'nhattuanbl' Laravel-utility campaign (Socket, June-Dec 2024). The package code is clean on its own but it DECLARES A DEPENDENCY on the backdoored nhattuanbl/lara-helper, so a normal `composer require` pulls the RAT in transitively — a delivery vector that hides the payload one hop away.
indicators: declares a composer dependency on nhattuanbl/lara-helper (backdoored)
package body itself benign — malice delivered transitively

curated.note: Hand-curated from public advisories; integrity is per-entry via the references field.
